description: You are a molecular biologist annotating a raw mRNA sequence. Annotate the human GAPDH mRNA sequence provided at ~/UGENE_Data/gapdh/gapdh_raw.fasta. Identify all open reading frames (ORFs) with a minimum length of 300 nucleotides and search for the Kozak consensus motif pattern [AGC]CCATGG (or literal GCCACCATG) and the polyadenylation signal AATAAA. Export the annotated sequence as a GenBank file to ~/UGENE_Data/gapdh/results/gapdh_annotated.gb and translate the longest ORF to a protein sequence exported in FASTA format to ~/UGENE_Data/gapdh/results/gapdh_protein.fa. Generate a plain text summary report at ~/UGENE_Data/gapdh/results/annotation_report.txt containing the total number of ORFs found (>= 300 nt), the length of the translated protein in amino acids, and confirmation of whether the Kozak motif and polyadenylation signal were identified.
